judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports fully cover all four expectations with accurate file/line citations; they are comparable, with Report 2 adding the staging-can-assume-prod trust overlap and Report 1 adding more depth on missing checkout/permissions.

report_1.evidence: Line 2: `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`... This line should be removed entirely from `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars`... Line 3: `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"`... Should be removed entirely... Line 4: `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"`... Should be removed entirely... Both lines need to be removed entirely from `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars`... Line 7: `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN`... shouldn't be recreated in prod's config

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:2` — `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`... Should be `"false"`... `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"`... Should be `"false"`... `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"`... should be empty/removed from prod entirely... `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK`... This should either be emptied

report_1.evidence: the API base URL in the frontend bundle that would ship to production is the literal string `https://staging-api.example.com`, baked in during the one and only build, because the build is always `--mode staging`... and the exact same image digest built for staging is promoted to prod rather than rebuilt

report_2.evidence: the build step statically bakes `VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://staging-api.example.com` into the JS bundle, unconditionally... The same digest — the same already-built image/bundle — is passed to both `deploy-staging`... and `deploy-prod`... unless the pipeline is also changed to build once per target environment

report_1.evidence: docs/prod-runbook.md says "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but... this claim can't be confirmed. Three migration files exist... but there's no `alembic.ini` and no visible invocation of them anywhere in the workflow

report_2.evidence: docs/prod-runbook.md:6 states "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but that logic (if it exists) would have to live inside the missing `scripts/deploy.sh`... I can't confirm from this repo that a production database has been provisioned at all, or that migrations would actually run against it

report_1.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` is an unedited copy of staging's line — still `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"`... This has to be corrected to `"repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production"`

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` still sets `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"`... It must read `"repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production"` to match the GitHub OIDC `sub` claim that the `deploy-prod` job... will actually present
